talk_metadata.institution_or_pharma: University of California, Davis / Luminance Biosciences
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "We saw more lesions with our F-18 peptide than with FDG-PET, but the patient already knew they had metastatic disease. So we asked ourselves what can we do with this information to actually help a patient?"
Speaker noted: "No SAEs, no adverse events" despite single dose administration.

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Limited tumor penetration and rapid renal clearance may reduce delivered dose to deep tumor sites
vulnerability_identified.ghost_responder_admission: Only a few patients showed SUV drop; most showed stable disease
vulnerability_identified.hidden_tox_signal: Kidney dose‑limiting organ noted, but no explicit toxicity reported

trial_dilution_risk.trial_name_or_nct: Phase I αvβ6 gallium/lutetium theranostic trial (pancreatic cancer)
trial_dilution_risk.target_biology: Integrin αvβ6 overexpression in pancreatic and other solid tumors
trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: Enrolled all patients with advanced disease regardless of quantitative αvβ6 expression or tumor burden heterogeneity
trial_dilution_risk.missing_biomarker: Pre‑treatment αvβ6 PET SUV threshold to stratify responders
trial_dilution_risk.statistical_concern: Single‑dose escalation without control arm limits efficacy interpretation

competitive_moat_weakness.vulnerable_ip_or_chemistry: Peptide‑based αvβ6 ligand (A20FMDV2 derivative) with DOTA chelator
competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Speaker disclosed the peptide sequence and albumin‑binding moiety, suggesting limited novel scaffold beyond the peptide backbone
competitive_moat_weakness.scale_or_manufacturing_bottleneck: In‑house synthesis and radiolabeling at academic site may limit large‑scale production
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Single‑dose protocol and lack of biomarker‑driven patient selection could dilute therapeutic signal

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed high‑resolution PET images detecting hundreds of lesions, then concluded that the agent "didn't help the patient" because the disease was already known, contradicting the diagnostic value of the imaging

crispro_opportunity.description: Develop a quantitative αvβ6 PET‑based cutoff to select patients likely to receive therapeutic dose
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said patients were eligible if SUV was two‑times normal, but many lesions were only marginally above background
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can model tumor uptake kinetics and predict optimal SUV thresholds for therapeutic benefit
external_validation_needed: ClinicalTrials.gov: retrieve full protocol for the αvβ6 trial
PubMed: pharmacokinetic modeling of αvβ6 peptides

crispro_opportunity.description: Combine αvβ6 lutetium therapy with PARP inhibitor to exploit DNA damage synergy
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker mentioned a paper under review combining the agent with olaparib
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can simulate DNA repair pathway inhibition effects on radiopharmaceutical efficacy
external_validation_needed: Check ongoing trials of PARP + radioligand combos

cited_competitors.name: Lutathera
cited_competitors.context: Reference to approved somatostatin receptor radioligand therapy

cited_competitors.name: Pluvicto
cited_competitors.context: Reference to approved PSMA radioligand therapy

unresolved_questions: Will multi‑dose regimens improve efficacy without increasing renal toxicity?
Can the αvβ6 agent effectively treat brain metastases after BBB disruption?

rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "We can inject small amounts of radiolabeled antibody – three milligrams total – and still get clear imaging"
Speaker noted: "The failed ADC trial (Rova‑T) was stopped because of toxicity, not because the antibody was bad"

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: ADC linker‑payload caused neurotoxicity unrelated to target binding
vulnerability_identified.ghost_responder_admission: Some patients responded before trial stopped
vulnerability_identified.hidden_tox_signal: Speaker emphasized toxicity was the reason for trial halt

trial_dilution_risk.trial_name_or_nct: DLL3 PET/theranostic pilot in small‑cell lung cancer
trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: Enrolled based on histology without confirming DLL3 positivity by PET before therapy
trial_dilution_risk.missing_biomarker: Pre‑treatment DLL3 PET to confirm target density
trial_dilution_risk.statistical_concern: Very small patient numbers (≤7) limit statistical power

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Speaker disclosed that Novartis will not pursue SC16 further and is developing a new human antibody (010), indicating limited IP protection on the original scaffold
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Reliance on a single low‑abundance target (DLL3) may limit broader applicability

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed that DLL3 PET lit up only two lesions out of 74 on FDG, yet concluded that the agent provides a decisive guide for biopsy and treatment despite low overall lesion detection

key_data_gaps: No survival or response data for therapeutic arm
Limited comparison to standard imaging modalities

crispro_opportunity.description: Redevelop the SC16 scaffold with improved affinity and reduced immunogenicity for DLL3 theranostics
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker mentioned the company is moving to a new human antibody (010) after abandoning SC16
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can predict affinity maturation pathways and assess off‑target binding risk
external_validation_needed: Patent filings on SC16 and 010 antibodies

crispro_opportunity.description: Combine DLL3 radioligand with PARP inhibitor (olaparib) to exploit DNA repair deficiency in neuroendocrine tumors
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker referenced a paper under review combining the agent with olaparib
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can model synthetic lethality between radiopharmaceutical‑induced DNA breaks and PARP inhibition
external_validation_needed: ClinicalTrials.gov: search for DLL3 + PARP inhibitor combos

unresolved_questions: What is the optimal dosing schedule for DLL3 radioligand to balance efficacy and renal exposure?
Can DLL3 expression be pharmacologically up‑regulated in low‑expressing tumors?

talk_metadata.talk_title: Next‑generation targets, isotopes, and combination strategies for radioligand therapy
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "We have 94 new trials in 2025, showing an explosion of the field"
Speaker noted: "Target homogeneity is a key criterion; we use spatial transcriptomics to select candidates"

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Cross‑fire effect may be limited by short beta range of lutetium in tumors where FAP is peripheral
vulnerability_identified.ghost_responder_admission: Tumor control observed but not complete regression in some models

trial_dilution_risk.trial_name_or_nct: Phase I FXX‑489 (Fab‑targeting) trial in pancreatic cancer
trial_dilution_risk.target_biology: Fibroblast activation protein (FAP) in tumor stroma
trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: Enrolled patients based on histology without confirming FAP density or uniformity
trial_dilution_risk.missing_biomarker: Pre‑treatment FAP PET to stratify high‑density stromal tumors
trial_dilution_risk.statistical_concern: Early‑phase single‑arm design limits efficacy conclusions

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Speaker highlighted that DOTA chelator contributes to binding, suggesting the core Fab may be less unique
competitive_moat_weakness.scale_or_manufacturing_bottleneck: Fab production at scale can be more complex than small peptides
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Reliance on stromal target may dilute tumor‑cell specific dose, risking lower therapeutic index

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker demonstrated robust DNA damage in fibroblasts (off‑target cells) after FXX‑489 treatment, yet promoted this as a therapeutic advantage without addressing potential stromal toxicity

key_data_gaps: Comparison of beta vs alpha emitters for FAP targeting

crispro_opportunity.description: Incorporate FAP PET quantification as a mandatory eligibility criterion to enrich for high‑FAP tumors
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker admitted current trial enrolls based on histology without biomarker confirmation
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can simulate patient‑level dosimetry based on FAP PET SUV to predict therapeutic benefit

crispro_opportunity.description: Combine FAP‑targeted lutetium with DNA‑PK inhibitor to enhance radiosensitization
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker discussed DNA‑PK screens identifying it as a radiosensitizer for lutetium DOTATATE
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can model synergistic DNA‑damage response inhibition with radioligand therapy
external_validation_needed: Identify clinical‑grade DNA‑PK inhibitors in development

cited_competitors.name: Yttrium‑90
cited_competitors.context: Mentioned as alternative beta emitter with longer path length

unresolved_questions: What is the optimal isotope (beta vs alpha) for stromal targets like FAP?
Can pre‑targeting strategies mitigate off‑target fibroblast irradiation?
